reforms.description: The government plans to phase in fiscal measures to raise medium-term growth and stimulate the economy, which includes higher spending on innovation and infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased spending on innovation is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The package includes measures to increase spending on innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing innovation funding, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: This package foresees inter alia higher spending on innovation and infrastructure.

reforms.description: Germany has implemented measures to improve the framework conditions for innovation by removing administrative burdens on entrepreneurship and abolishing some tax expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing administrative burdens, the reforms aim to enhance the entrepreneurial environment, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Ongoing projects have been reported, indicating that measures are in place.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant step towards improving the innovation environment, although it is part of a broader set of measures rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Remove administrative burdens on entrepreneurship. Ongoing projects, such as strengthened regulatory impact analysis in some states.

reforms.description: The government established a timetable for all government-funded innovation programmes and committed to eliminating ineffective subsidies, aiming to enhance the efficiency of public research funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining innovation funding and removing ineffective subsidies, the reform is expected to improve resource allocation and enhance the overall effectiveness of public research investments.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to a timetable and the elimination of ineffective subsidies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how innovation funding is managed, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of public investment in research and development.
reforms.source_quote: Establish timetable for all government-funded innovation programmes and eliminate ineffective subsidies.

reforms.description: The 'Exzellenzinitiative' is a new programme co-funded by the federal government and states, aimed at improving performance incentives for universities by tendering funds for high-quality postgraduate teaching schools and research projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing high-quality research and teaching, the initiative aims to enhance the overall educational and research output of universities, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in how funding is allocated to universities, focusing on quality and performance, which is crucial for enhancing the education system's effectiveness.
reforms.source_quote: A new programme ('Exzellenzinitiative') co-funded by the federal government and the states, foresees the tendering of funds for the highest-quality post-graduate teaching schools and research projects submitted by universities.

reforms.description: The introduction of student fees for tertiary education, coupled with a loan scheme and income-contingent repayments, aims to provide additional financial resources to universities while ensuring that students can defer payments until after graduation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding for universities, this reform aims to enhance the quality of education and align it more closely with labor market needs, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: 5 of 16 states are planning to introduce student fees amounting up to €1000 per year of study by 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the introduction of student fees is a narrower reform focused on funding rather than a comprehensive overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: 5 of 16 states are planning to introduce student fees amounting up to €1000 per year of study by 2008.

reforms.description: The German government plans to introduce a new income replacement scheme (Elterngeld) that will replace the existing Erziehungsgeld, providing two-thirds of earnings foregone due to reductions in labor supply after childbirth for a year.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While it may incentivize some parents to remain in the labor force, it could also discourage highly qualified individuals from working due to the high income replacement rate.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to introduce a new income replacement scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in family support policy, aiming to improve labor supply incentives for parents, particularly mothers.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to introduce a new income replacement scheme (Elterngeld) that replaces over one year two thirds of earnings foregone due to reductions in labour supply after childbirth.

reforms.description: The Ich-AG support scheme was introduced to provide unconditional subsidies to unemployed individuals who decide to start their own businesses, although it has since been modified to require a business plan for eligibility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support for entrepreneurship, the scheme encourages the creation of new businesses, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was associated with very high take-up rates among unemployed individuals.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the Ich-AG scheme represents a significant policy shift towards supporting entrepreneurship among the unemployed, with potential long-term benefits for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Ich-AG support scheme granted unconditional subsidies to the unemployed who decide to set up their own business.

reforms.description: The German government aims to increase the efficiency of the education system by linking government funding for universities to output indicators and granting them more independence, including the ability to introduce student fees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education efficiency and aligning funding with performance can enhance the quality of graduates, thereby increasing the skilled workforce necessary for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to reform university funding to enhance educational outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how universities are funded, potentially leading to improved educational outcomes and a more skilled workforce.
reforms.source_quote: Allocation of government funding to universities should be more strongly oriented toward output indicators.

reforms.description: The new government has increased government R&D spending as part of a broader fiscal strategy to stimulate the economy and improve growth prospects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has phased in fiscal measures including increased R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The package comprises...increases in government R&D spending as well as in transport infrastructure.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase R&D spending to enhance long-term growth prospects, aiming to stimulate investment and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to foster innovation and improve productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to increase R&D spending as part of its fiscal strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in R&D spending is a significant step towards enhancing innovation capacity, although it is part of a broader fiscal strategy.
reforms.source_quote: some of the measures –such as the increase in government R&D spending– also aim at raising longer term growth prospects.

reforms.description: Germany is aiming to enhance the utilization of public-private partnerships (PPPs) for infrastructure financing, following the removal of regulatory obstacles that previously hindered private investment in this area.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: PPPs are expected to leverage private sector efficiency and innovation in infrastructure projects, potentially leading to better service delivery and cost savings for the government.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation which came into force in mid-2005 removed regulatory obstacles for PPPs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in how infrastructure projects are financed, aiming to improve efficiency and service delivery through private sector involvement.
reforms.source_quote: Public sector authorities in Germany are aiming at greater utilisation of public-private sector partnerships (PPPs) to finance infrastructure, based on the evaluation of costs and benefits over the life cycle of investment projects.
